title: CV
text:
Current Position
Pre-Doctoral Fellow, Nova SBE, 2025-2026.
I work under the supervision of Professor Pedro S. Martins on labour topics, supporting research projects through literature reviews, data cleaning, data analysis, and drafting. I am affiliated with the Nova Public Policy Institute and the Economics of Education Knowledge Center.

title: CV
text:
Education

MSc in Economics, FEP, University of Porto, 2021-2023.
Exchange semester, Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich.
BSc in Management, DEGEIT, University of Aveiro, 2018-2021.

title: CV
text:
Research Experience

Pre-Doctoral Fellow, Nova SBE, Lisbon.
Intern and Consultant, International Labour Organization, Geneva.
Master’s thesis: “Regional Labour Market Concentration and Wages: Evidence from Portugal.”

title: CV
text:
Skills

Administrative microdata: employer-employee longitudinal data, firm accounting data, public employment services data, and education data.
Software: Stata, Python, LaTeX.
Languages: Portuguese, English.

title: Research
text:

“Is the future of work childless? Self-employment, contract type and fertility”, with Judite Goncalves and Pedro S. Martins.
“Minimum wages and retention”, with Jonathan Thomas, Andrew Snell, and Pedro S. Martins.
“The anatomy of private-sector strikes: firm predictors, worker participation, and economic consequences”, with Pedro S. Martins.

title: Welcome
text:
I am a pre-doctoral fellow at Nova SBE, affiliated with the Nova Public Policy Institute and the Economics of Education Knowledge Center.
I am an applied microeconomist interested in labour economics, migration, education, and public policy.
enzo.almeida@novasbe.pt
View CV

title: Welcome
text:
About
My current research work uses administrative microdata to study labour markets, firms, families, and public policy. I am especially interested in how labour-market institutions and migration shape workers’ outcomes and economic adjustment.
Before joining Nova SBE, I worked as an intern and consultant at the International Labour Organization, contributing to employment impact assessments of public infrastructure investments.

title: Welcome
text:
Research Interests

Labour economics
Migration
Applied microeconomics
Economics of education
Public policy
